title: Aggregate measurement over a custom entity: one measure group, one measure, one dimension attribute
instruction: In the sandbox model, build an analytics star-schema slice. Ground it first with get_knowledge(topic="aggregate-measurements"). Create: (1) a table DemoRequestFact (label @TaxTransactionInquiry:HeaderNote, table group Main) with fields RequestCode (String, EDT Num, mandatory), RequestType (String, EDT Name) and DaysToClose (Integer), plus a unique alternate-key index RequestFactIdx on RequestCode; (2) a data entity DemoRequestFactEntity over that table (public, EntityCategory=Master, RequestCode as the key) - the fact source, so the entity-store refresh reads a denormalised shape; (3) an aggregate measurement DemoRequestMeasure (d365fo_file objectType "aggregate-measurement") with Usage=StagedEntityStore and exactly one measure group over ConDemoRequestFactEntity that has one dimension attribute on RequestType and one measure AvgDaysToClose with AggregateFunction=Avg over DaysToClose. Modelling the measure group over the raw table instead of the entity FAILS this case. SCOPE LIMIT: deploying/refreshing the entity store is a runtime operation and is out of scope - the case scores the metadata. All objects must build with zero BP errors.
